articles.title: Fun Spot America Permanently Closes Atlanta Location on August 2
articles.summary: Fun Spot America has announced the permanent closure of its Atlanta-area park, with the final day of operation set for August 2, 2026. The closure raises questions about the future of the ArieForce One roller coaster, one of the park's marquee attractions. This is a significant development for the FEC and amusement park industry, signaling continued pressure on mid-tier regional parks.

articles.title: AAMA Executive Vice President Pete Gustafson Resigns After 10 Years
articles.summary: The American Amusement Machine Association has announced that Executive Vice President Pete Gustafson has resigned effective June 28, 2026, after a decade in the role. Gustafson has been a central figure in the coin-op and amusement machine industry, and his departure marks a significant leadership transition for the trade group. FEC operators and arcade suppliers who rely on AAMA for advocacy and industry standards will want to watch for news on his successor.

articles.title: DIVR Labs to Debut New VR Game 'Castle Crusade' for Bowling Centers at Bowl Expo 2026
articles.summary: DIVR Labs will showcase Castle Crusade, a new multiplayer game for its self-service InstaVR platform, at Bowl Expo 2026 in Nashville this week. The InstaVR system requires no dedicated staff to operate, making it an attractive revenue-add for bowling centers looking to diversify without increasing labor costs. FEC and bowling operators attending Bowl Expo should make Booth #875 a priority stop.

articles.title: Semnox Solutions Deploys Parafait Platform at New Multi-Attraction FEC K&K Adventure Zone
articles.summary: K&K Adventure Zone in Prince Frederick, Maryland, has opened with a full Semnox Parafait deployment including LuminOS game readers, self-service kiosks, and a centralized POS system, installed in partnership with Betson Enterprises. The venue features arcades, laser tag, VR, mini-golf, and a laser maze — a classic multi-attraction FEC model. The case study offers practical insight for operators evaluating cashless and self-service technology for similarly complex venues.

articles.title: Intercard Installs Cashless System at 23rd Speedpark FEC Location in France
articles.summary: Intercard has completed its 23rd cashless technology installation for French amusement operator Speedpark, this time at a new 54,000-square-foot FEC in Rennes featuring go-karts, bowling, laser tag, and arcade games. The milestone underscores the depth of long-term supplier-operator relationships in the FEC tech space. For operators evaluating cashless providers, Intercard's scale across a single multi-location operator is a strong proof-of-concept.

articles.title: Sony Pictures Makes $100M Strategic Investment in Immersive Venue Company Cosm
articles.summary: Sony Pictures Entertainment is acquiring a minority stake in Cosm, a location-based entertainment technology company that builds immersive shared experience venues, in a $100 million deal. The investment will fund expansion of Cosm's LBE footprint and technology development across sports and entertainment. The move signals growing mainstream media investment in the LBE and immersive entertainment space, raising the competitive bar for FEC operators.

articles.title: Hasbro-Branded Family Entertainment Center Coming to Pigeon Forge in Summer 2027
articles.summary: A new Hasbro-branded family entertainment center is set to open in Pigeon Forge, Tennessee, in summer 2027, adding a major IP-driven FEC to one of the country's top family tourism destinations. The project reflects the growing trend of toy and entertainment brands extending into location-based experiences. FEC operators and developers should take note of how licensed IP continues to drive venue concepts and consumer traffic.

articles.title: Little Lion Entertainment to Open Large-Scale Arcade Arena on London's Southbank in September
articles.summary: Little Lion Entertainment will launch Arcade Arena London in September, a purpose-built live gaming venue featuring Chaos Karts, an immersive Pac-Man experience developed with Bandai Namco, and a laser tag and drone combat experience called Alien Invasion. The venue is designed for repeatable visits and high-volume urban foot traffic. It represents a compelling model for large-format, IP-partnered FEC concepts in major cities.

articles.title: Fun Spot America Atlanta Closing Leaves Future of ArieForce One Coaster Uncertain
articles.summary: Following Fun Spot America's announcement of its Atlanta park closure, Coaster101 reports that the fate of the ArieForce One roller coaster — widely regarded as one of the best steel coasters in North America — remains unclear. The situation highlights the financial risks associated with capital-intensive attractions at standalone amusement parks. Operators and investors watching the mid-tier regional park segment should follow this story closely.

articles.title: Cineplex Expands Playdium FEC Brand with New Vaughan Mills Location
articles.summary: Cineplex is bringing a new Playdium location to Vaughan Mills shopping mall in Ontario, Canada, continuing its strategy of placing entertainment centers within retail destinations. Playdium venues typically feature arcades, redemption games, and other FEC attractions, offering a proven model for mall-based entertainment. This expansion is relevant for operators and developers exploring retail co-location strategies.

articles.title: Slick City Action Park Opens New Location at Potomac Mills Mall
articles.summary: Slick City Action Park, an indoor slide and active entertainment concept, has opened a new location at Potomac Mills in Northern Virginia, continuing the brand's mall-based expansion strategy. The opening adds to a growing roster of active entertainment concepts targeting retail mall anchor positions. FEC operators evaluating active entertainment formats for mall locations will find this a relevant market data point.

articles.title: Big Air Indoor Trampoline Park Opens New Location in Glendale, Arizona
articles.summary: Big Air, a national indoor trampoline park chain, has opened a new location in Glendale, Arizona, expanding its footprint in the Southwest market. The opening reflects continued consumer demand for active indoor entertainment, particularly in hot-weather markets where outdoor options are limited in summer. Operators in warm climates should note the seasonal demand drivers supporting indoor active entertainment growth.

articles.title: UK Heatwave Drives Consumers Toward Indoor Entertainment Venues
articles.summary: A record-breaking heatwave across the UK is pushing consumers to seek out indoor entertainment options, creating a short-term demand surge for FEC and indoor attraction operators. The trend reinforces the value proposition of climate-controlled indoor entertainment venues as weather-resilient businesses. UK-based FEC operators are well-positioned to capitalize on walk-in traffic during extreme heat events.

articles.title: Kokomo Joe's FEC Adding Multi-Level Go-Kart Track
articles.summary: Kokomo Joe's, a family entertainment center, is investing in a new multi-level go-kart track, a capital improvement that reflects the strong ROI operators have seen from elevated karting formats. Multi-level tracks have become an increasingly popular draw for FECs competing with larger entertainment destinations. Operators considering go-kart upgrades will find this a timely example of the format's continued appeal.

articles.title: Drone Light Shows Emerge as a Sustainable Alternative to Fireworks at Parks and FECs
articles.summary: A detailed industry analysis explores how drone light shows are replacing traditional fireworks at amusement parks and entertainment venues, driven by noise concerns, environmental regulations, and community pushback. The shift has significant operational and budgetary implications for FEC operators who use fireworks or spectaculars as part of their event programming. Venues planning Fourth of July or seasonal events should evaluate drone show providers as a forward-looking alternative.

articles.title: Chuck E. Cheese Releases 29-Country Research on Birthday Party Guest Lists
articles.summary: Chuck E. Cheese has published research spanning 29 countries examining how parents manage birthday party guest lists, providing data-driven insight into a core revenue category for FECs. The research highlights global variations in party size preferences and social dynamics that affect birthday party bookings. FEC operators dependent on birthday party revenue will find the data useful for refining marketing and package offerings.

articles.title: Fort Worth's Tornado Terry's Family Amusement Center Closing After Three Decades
articles.summary: Tornado Terry's Family Amusement Center in Fort Worth, Texas, is shutting down after approximately 30 years of operation, marking the end of a long-running independent FEC. The closure reflects ongoing competitive and economic pressures facing legacy independent operators. The story is a cautionary reminder of the challenges smaller, single-location FECs face in today's market.

articles.title: PICO and STYLY Partner to Scale VR Content Distribution Across Global LBE Venues
articles.summary: STYLY, a spatial layer platform evolving into a global LBE content distribution network, has selected PICO 4 Ultra Enterprise headsets for use across nearly all of its location-based experience projects. The partnership focuses on ease of use for non-technical staff and accessibility for guests of all ages, two critical factors for FEC VR deployments. Operators evaluating VR hardware and content platforms should take note of this emerging distribution model.

articles.title: High Tide VR Attraction Near Bethany Beach Offers Coastal FEC Alternative
articles.summary: High Tide VR, located near Bethany Beach, Delaware, is profiled as a distinctive entertainment option in a beach tourism market typically dominated by outdoor attractions. The venue demonstrates how VR-focused FECs can carve out a niche in seasonal tourism destinations. Operators in resort and coastal markets may find the model relevant for addressing demand on rainy or off-peak days.

articles.title: Chance Rides Emphasizes Long-Term Service and Parts Support as Key to Operator ROI
articles.summary: Ride manufacturer Chance Rides is making the case that post-installation service, parts availability, and technical support are as important as the initial ride purchase for maximizing operator ROI. The company argues that long-term reliability — not opening day — determines a ride's true value. FEC and amusement park operators evaluating ride suppliers should factor in lifetime service commitments alongside upfront costs.

articles.title: New Adventure Park Opens in Atlanta Amid Fun Spot Closure
articles.summary: A new adventure park described as unlike any other in the country has opened in Atlanta, arriving just as Fun Spot America announces the closure of its Atlanta-area facility. The juxtaposition highlights the dynamic and competitive nature of the Atlanta FEC market. Operators and investors tracking regional market shifts will find both stories relevant in tandem.

articles.title: Masti Zone Opens New Family Entertainment Destination in Greater Noida West, India
articles.summary: Masti Zone has launched a new family entertainment destination at PKS Town Central in Greater Noida West, expanding the FEC footprint in one of India's fastest-growing urban markets. The opening reflects the global expansion of the FEC concept into emerging markets with large young-family demographics. Suppliers and investors with international ambitions should monitor India's rapidly developing indoor entertainment sector.

articles.title: Poland Opens Immersive Media and VR Attraction in Karkonosze Region
articles.summary: A new immersive media and VR attraction has launched in Piechowice in Poland's Karkonosze mountain region, adding a technology-driven entertainment draw to a scenic tourism destination. The project illustrates how immersive VR attractions are being integrated into non-traditional, tourism-driven markets across Europe. FEC operators and attraction developers interested in European expansion will find the format and location strategy noteworthy.

articles.title: Arizona's Top 10 Indoor Entertainment Attractions for 2026 Ranked
articles.summary: AZ Big Media has published its annual ranking of the top 10 indoor entertainment attractions in Arizona for 2026, offering a snapshot of which venue types and brands are resonating with consumers in a major Sun Belt market. The list provides competitive intelligence for operators in the region and highlights the formats driving the most consumer interest. FEC operators in Arizona and similar hot-weather markets should review which concepts are earning top placement.

articles.title: Chalk & Steel Co. Plans New Indoor Entertainment Venue in Keller, Texas
articles.summary: Chalk & Steel Co. is bringing a new indoor entertainment venue to Keller, Texas, a growing Dallas-Fort Worth suburb with strong family demographics. The project adds to a busy pipeline of new FEC openings across the DFW metro area. Operators and developers tracking market saturation and opportunity in Texas's major metros will want to note the continued influx of new concepts.
